chunk_summary: Guidelines for adolescent energy needs and food-group portions are provided, tailored to age, sex, activity, and growth phenotypes. Adjustments are made for growth spurts, training, or adiposity, with practical Indian food examples and sugar reduction strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand energy requirements for adolescents based on age, sex, and activity levels.
Learn to prescribe food-group portions using NIN balanced-diet guidelines.
Identify strategies to manage underweight, growth spurts, or excess adiposity in adolescents.
Apply WHO free sugar recommendations to reduce sugar intake in adolescents.
key_facts: Energy needs: Boys 13–15 years ~2860 kcal/day, girls 13–15 years ~2400 kcal/day; boys 16–18 years ~3320 kcal/day, girls 16–18 years ~2500 kcal/day.
Adjust energy intake by ±10–20% for growth velocity, training, or adiposity phenotype.
Daily food-group minimums: cereals/millets at each meal, pulses/legumes 2–3 portions, milk/curd 3–5 portions, vegetables 3 portions (1 green leafy), fruits 1–2 portions, nuts/oilseeds daily.
Visible fats: 20–50 g/day depending on energy needs; avoid fried snacks for catch-up nutrition.
WHO free sugar ceiling: <10% of energy, ideally <5%, with a focus on eliminating sugar-sweetened beverages.
Document and review 1–2 actionable dietary swaps for school or hostel settings.
reference_quote: "WHO free sugar ceiling: <10% of energy, ideally <5%, with a focus on eliminating sugar-sweetened beverages."
